hypothesis: Baseline Phase 2 with foundation Axis weights + 30 PU bid
notes: 94% Allied — Axis foundation weights too passive, not learning

hypothesis: Random Axis weights will explore more aggressively with +30 PU budget, dropping Allied win rate toward sweet spot
notes: Immediate drop from 94% to 82%. Random Axis with +30 PU bid is more competitive. Need 50+ iterations to confirm trend.

hypothesis: Higher Axis entropy (0.05 vs 0.02) will force diverse strategies, preventing Axis from collapsing to one predictable pattern that Allied easily counters
notes: Allied dropped from 86% to 80% at iteration 15. Axis exploring more with higher entropy + random init + 30 PU bid + 3x epochs. Need 50+ iterations to confirm.

hypothesis: Increasing Axis bid from +30 to +38 PUs will give Axis enough economic advantage to break through the 83% plateau. Germany at 54 PUs/turn (vs standard 37) should enable a serious Barbarossa push.
notes: Early result at iteration 15: 82%. Previous experiment plateaued at 83% with +30 bid. The extra 8 PUs should compound over rounds. Need 50+ iterations to see if Axis uses the extra budget to create real pressure.

hypothesis: Axis 3x VC reward (6.0 vs 2.0) will incentivize aggressive pushes toward Moscow/London/capitals instead of scattered skirmishing. This MACRO change addresses the root cause: Axis has money but no strategic direction.
variable: rust_engine/src/lib.rs (Axis VC reward multiplier in step_single + step_all)
notes: 3 consecutive MICRO experiments plateaued at 83%. Switching to MACRO. Axis gets 3x reward for VC captures, unchanged for Allied. Should produce more aggressive/directed Axis play.

hypothesis: Monitor check: Axis 3x VC reward (Exp #4) is working — win rate dropped to 74%, approaching sweet spot. No change needed.
notes: Exp #4 (MACRO: Axis 3x VC reward) is the first change to break below 80%. Allied at 74% after 175 iterations / 69k games. Approaching sweet spot (55-70%). Let it continue training.

hypothesis: Increasing Axis PPO epochs from 3x to 5x will give Axis more learning per game, pushing Allied win rate from 76% into the 55-70% sweet spot. Combined with 3x VC reward + random init + 38 bid + 0.05 entropy.
notes: Speed dropped from 5.4k to 4.1k sps due to more GPU work. Allied at 80% early — need 50+ iterations. If this drops below 70%, next experiment triggers LEAGUE TRAINING (highest human priority).

hypothesis: LEAGUE TRAINING: Allied faces diverse Axis opponents (50% current, 30% past snapshots, 20% random). Prevents strategy collapse. League activates at iteration 100 (after 2 snapshots). This is human priority #1.

notes: MACRO experiment implementing human priority #1. League pool stores Axis snapshots every 50 iterations. Random league model loaded each snapshot. Should produce more robust Allied strategies that generalize to different play styles (including James's dad).

notes: League active immediately with 10 diverse snapshots. Allied at 81% at iter 10 — need 50+ iters to see full effect. The foundation snapshots represent different Axis skill levels from 282k games of training.

hypothesis: Monitoring: League active, Allied plateaued at 83%. Considered increasing terminal reward from ±100 to ±500, but decided NOT to interrupt overnight run. Optimizer momentum would be lost. Better to let it accumulate 1.7M+ games and make changes in the morning.

Overnight run should reach iter 2000 / ~900k games this run / ~1.7M total. NEXT MORNING: try ±500 terminal reward or observation compression.

hypothesis: Monitoring: Allied slowly dropping (84→83→82%) with league active. Trend is correct, just slow. Don't interrupt overnight run.
notes: 1.1M total games. Iter 640/2000. League active with ~8 snapshots. Win rate dropping ~1% per 200 iters. ETA to 70%: ~2400 more iters (not this run). Morning plan: increase terminal reward or compress observations.

The slow convergence suggests the 8.9M param model may be near its capacity for this observation size.

(3) If still stuck, try SAC instead of PPO.

notes: 1.59M total games. Iter 1775/2000. ~27 league snapshots. Morning experiment prepared: change ±100 to ±500 terminal reward at 8 locations in rust_engine/src/lib.rs, rebuild, resume from final checkpoint.

hypothesis: Terminal reward ±100→±500 makes win/loss 5x more important than VC accumulation. Should push both agents to focus on actually winning, not just territory grabbing. Resumed from overnight final checkpoint with 30-snapshot league.
notes: IMMEDIATE IMPACT: Allied dropped from 80%→75% within first 15 iterations. Resumed from selfplay_final.pt (iter 2000, 850k games, 30 league snapshots). Running 4000 iters. Total all-time: ~1.68M+ games. This is the breakthrough we've been waiting for.

hypothesis: Monitoring: ±500 terminal reward bounced back to 80% after initial 75% drop. Expected — critic needs 500-1000 iters to recalibrate value estimates to new reward scale. Let it train.
notes: 1.86M total games. Iter 425/4000. The 75%→80% bounce is the optimizer recalibrating to 5x reward scale. Real effect won't be visible until iter 800+. Training healthy at 4.2k sps.

hypothesis: Monitoring: ±500 reward settled at 81%. Same plateau. About to cross 2M total games. The 80-81% ceiling has persisted for ~1M games across 6 experiments — this is an architectural limit, not hyperparameter.
notes: 1.98M total games. Plateau at 80-81% is definitive across experiments #4-18. MORNING DISCUSSION NEEDED: (1) Compress observation 16k→2k (remove distant/irrelevant territories), (2) Double network size 512→1024 hidden, (3) Add attention mechanism for territory relevance, (4) Try SAC algorithm. These are architectural changes that need user review.

hypothesis: Extreme Axis bid +60 PUs (Germany 65, Japan 51, Italy 22) as stress test. If even this doesn't break 81%, the ceiling is architectural. If it does, the agent just needs a harder opponent.
delta: 0.0 (just started, bid not yet in effect)
notes: 2.07M total games. Resumed from iter 900 checkpoint. The +60 bid only affects games after env reset — need ~50 iters for full effect. Germany at 65 PUs can buy fighter+3armor+4infantry every round. If Allied still at 80%+ after 200 iters, ceiling is confirmed architectural.

hypothesis: CONFIRMED: +60 PU bid had zero effect. Allied still 82% after 185 iterations. The 80-82% ceiling is definitively ARCHITECTURAL — the 8.9M param MLP on 16k observations has converged.
notes: 2.15M total games. 12 experiments across bid/reward/training/league all failed to break 80%. ARCHITECTURAL CHANGE REQUIRED. Options for morning: (1) Observation compression 16k→2k — remove 97 impassable/irrelevant territory slots, only encode frontline+factory+capital territories. Reduces first layer from 8.3M to ~1M params, letting remaining capacity focus on strategy. (2) Double hidden size 512→1024. (3) Add attention over territories. Recommend option 1 first — simpler and addresses the root cause (signal buried in noise).

hypothesis: Monitoring: 82% unchanged. 395 iters with +60 bid. Architectural ceiling confirmed. Training continues accumulating league snapshots (~20 now). No intervention — waiting for morning architectural discussion.
notes: ~2.24M total games. Iter 1295/4000. The training is still useful — each iteration adds league diversity and refines weights within the current capacity. But no win rate improvement possible without architectural change.
